macos_preflight.notes: Official online update was performed through the NETGEAR Nighthawk mobile app after browser setup/admin paths were blocked by 404 behavior. No static recovery IP was used.

readiness_observations.notes: Nighthawk app remained the reliable official management path immediately after update. Post-update app check reported firmware current with no newer firmware available. DHCP was maintained. Browser access via 192.168.1.1 and routerlogin.net initially remained abnormal/404, but the official App new-router setup flow later restored the browser local admin UI.

transfer.first_packet_timing: Nighthawk app update started at relative 0s after offering firmware update from 1.0.2.82 to 1.0.17.142.

post_upload.notes: Visible timeline: update started at 0s; app reported rebooting router at 2m19s; app reported update success at 4m01s. Firmware reached v1.0.17.142 and the app reported firmware current. DHCP was maintained. Browser local-admin paths initially remained abnormal/404 after update, but the official App new-router setup flow later restored the browser admin UI at 192.168.1.1/start.htm. Browser UI then showed firmware V1.0.17.142_2.0.100 and Nighthawk RAX40v2.

notes: Structured record generated from live session lab_tests/live_sessions/netgear_rax40_official_baseline_2026-05-16.md. This is an official Nighthawk app online firmware update baseline, not a Recovery Mode, Web Recovery, TFTP, or NMRP validation. Serial number, MAC addresses, Wi-Fi credentials, admin password, and private local paths were not recorded.
